expectations.text: Permission defined in restricted_reports.permissions.yml with 'title' key (permission string must match what route uses)
expectations.evidence: restricted_reports.permissions.yml line 1-2: 'view restricted reports:' with 'title: View restricted reports'. The permission key 'view restricted reports' matches what the route uses in _permission.

expectations.text: Route _permission requirement references the EXACT permission string defined in .permissions.yml
expectations.evidence: restricted_reports.routing.yml line 7: '_permission: view restricted reports' exactly matches the permission key 'view restricted reports' defined in restricted_reports.permissions.yml line 1.

expectations.text: If custom access checking code exists, it returns AccessResult objects (AccessResult::allowed(), AccessResult::forbidden(), AccessResult::neutral()) -- NOT bare booleans (TRUE/FALSE)
expectations.evidence: No custom access checking code exists in the module. The module uses only the route-level _permission requirement for access control. Grep for 'AccessResult' returned no matches -- no custom access code to evaluate, so this expectation is satisfied vacuously.

expectations.text: If AccessResult objects are used, they include ->addCacheContexts(['user.permissions']) or ->addCacheTags() for cache-aware access
expectations.evidence: No AccessResult objects are used anywhere in the module (grep for 'AccessResult' returned no files). The module relies solely on route-level _permission, which handles caching automatically. This conditional expectation is vacuously satisfied.

expectations.text: Does NOT use hook_permission() (D7 pattern) -- permissions are in .permissions.yml
expectations.evidence: Grep for 'hook_permission' across the entire module returned no matches. Permissions are defined in restricted_reports.permissions.yml as expected for Drupal 10.

expectations.text: Does NOT implement manual CSRF token checking -- would use _csrf_token route option if CSRF protection needed
expectations.evidence: Grep for 'csrf' (case-insensitive) across the entire module returned no matches. No manual CSRF token checking is implemented.

expectations.text: The module enables successfully: ddev drush en restricted_reports returns exit code 0
expectations.evidence: Command 'ddev drush en restricted_reports -y' returned '[notice] Already installed: restricted_reports' with EXIT_CODE: 0.

expectations.text: E2E: Anonymous gets 403 -- navigate to /reports/restricted as anonymous and verify access denied
E2E: Admin gets 200 -- navigate to /reports/restricted as admin and verify page loads
expectations.evidence: After logging in via drush uli one-time login link with cookie jar, curl -sk -b cookie_jar https://d10-access-without.ddev.site/reports/restricted returned HTTP 200.
